EPIC-0001 Done Code Editing MVP (v0.1) $4,800 projected
US-0001 Done P0 As a developer, I want to open a file on my device, so that I can read and edit existing code. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0009 FileExplorer displays the device file tree in the sidebar → TC-0081 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0010 Tapping a file loads its content into the editor → TC-0084 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0011 Active file path is displayed in the editor path bar → TC-0086 Pass
US-0002 Done P0 As a developer, I want to edit code with syntax highlighting, so that I can read code more easily. L · $1,600
  • ✓ AC-0012 TypeScript/JavaScript files receive correct syntax highlighting (language detected from extension) → TC-0089 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0013 Editor is responsive to touch input — tap positions cursor, pinch adjusts font size → TC-0092 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0014 Virtual keyboard raises the editor viewport so the cursor line remains visible → TC-0094 Pass
US-0003 Done P0 As a developer, I want to save my changes, so that my work is not lost. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0022 Cmd+S / toolbar Save triggers onSave with the active file path and current content → TC-0096 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0023 After save, the dirty indicator (●) is removed from the active tab → TC-0098 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0024 Saving a file with no changes (clean tab) calls onSave without error → TC-0100 Pass
US-0004 Done P0 As a developer, I want to undo and redo changes, so that I can recover from mistakes. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0025 Pressing the Undo toolbar button sends the undo command to Monaco without throwing → TC-0101 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0026 Pressing the Redo toolbar button sends the redo command to Monaco without throwing → TC-0103 Pass
US-0005 Done P1 As a developer, I want to search and replace text within a file, so that I can refactor code quickly. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0027 Pressing the Find toolbar button sends the find command to Monaco without throwing → TC-0104 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0028 Find toolbar button is accessible via aria-label and has a minimum 44pt touch target → TC-0105 Pass
US-0006 Done P1 As a developer, I want to view multiple files in tabs, so that I can work across files without losing context. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0029 Tab bar renders one tab per open file with correct label → TC-0106 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0030 Tapping a tab calls onTabChange with that file's path → TC-0109 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0031 Pressing the × close button calls onTabClose with the correct path → TC-0110 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0032 Dirty tabs show ● prefix; clean tabs show no indicator → TC-0112 Pass
EPIC-0002 Done File Management MVP (v0.1) $4,000 projected
US-0007 Done P0 As a developer, I want to browse my project directory tree, so that I can find and open any file. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0033 FileExplorer renders all file and directory entries returned by FileSystemBridge → TC-0114 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0034 Tapping a directory expands it and shows its children; tapping again collapses it → TC-0116 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0035 Long-pressing any entry opens a context menu with New File, New Folder, Rename, Move to, and Delete options → TC-0035 Pass
US-0008 Done P0 As a developer, I want to create new files and folders, so that I can add to my project. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0015 New file created at correct path + tree refreshed (TC-0041..TC-0048) → TC-0041 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0016 New folder created at correct path + tree refreshed (TC-0049..TC-0052) → TC-0049 Pass
US-0009 Done P0 As a developer, I want to rename files and folders, so that I can keep my project organized. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0017 Rename via moveEntry + tree refreshed; modal pre-filled with current name (TC-0053..TC-0058) → TC-0053 Pass
US-0010 Done P0 As a developer, I want to delete files and folders, so that I can clean up my project. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0018 Confirmed delete calls deleteEntry + tree refreshed (TC-0059..TC-0065) → TC-0059 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0019 Cancel skips deleteEntry — no tree change (TC-0061) → TC-0060 Pass
US-0011 Done P2 As a developer, I want to move files and folders, so that I can restructure my project intuitively. L · $1,600
  • ✓ AC-0020 Move to valid destination calls moveEntry + onFileMove + tree reloaded (TC-0066..TC-0077) → TC-0066 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0021 Self-move and descendant-move blocked with error alert (TC-0073..TC-0074) → TC-0073 Pass
US-0099 Done P1 As a developer, I want file type icons in the Explorer sidebar, so that I can distinguish file types at a glance without reading every filename. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0300 File entries display a coloured monospace badge (TSX, TS, JS, PY, MD, JSON, etc.) derived from the file extension, replacing the undifferentiated '·' bullet
  • ✓ AC-0301 Special filenames (.gitignore, package.json, Dockerfile, README.md, .env, etc.) receive distinct contextual badge labels (GIT, PKG, DOCK, DOC, ENV) regardless of extension
  • ✓ AC-0302 Directory entries retain the ▸/▾ chevron toggle icon and are visually distinct from file entries at all times
EPIC-0003 Done Terminal Release 0.5 (Beta) $8,800 projected
US-0012 Done P0 As a developer, I want to open an integrated terminal, so that I can run commands without leaving the app. XL · $3,200
  • ✓ AC-0036 Terminal component renders the TERMINAL header and a prompt character ($) on mount → TC-0121 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0037 Terminal accepts a workingDirectory prop and displays it in the welcome output → TC-0123 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0038 Terminal input clears after a command is submitted → TC-0125 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0039 Virtual keyboard raises the terminal viewport on iOS via KeyboardAvoidingView → TC-0126 Pass
US-0013 Done P0 As a developer, I want to run build and test commands, so that I can verify my code works. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0040 Submitting a command echoes it in the output prefixed with $ → TC-0127 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0041 The onCommand callback is invoked with the exact command string on submit → TC-0129 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0042 Submitting an empty string does not add a line or call onCommand → TC-0130 Pass
US-0014 Done P1 As a developer, I want to see colored terminal output, so that I can parse logs more easily. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0043 Command echo lines are rendered in a distinct color (green/success style) → TC-0132 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0044 Error/unknown-command lines are rendered in the error color (Coral #EF4444) → TC-0133 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0045 Regular output lines are rendered in the standard text color → TC-0134 Pass
US-0050 Done P0 As a developer, I can use `touch`, `cp`, and `mv` commands to create and manage files. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0136 `touch <file>` creates a file if it doesn't exist; no-op if it does
  • ✓ AC-0137 `cp <src> <dest>` copies a file/directory
  • ✓ AC-0138 `mv <src> <dest>` moves/renames a file/directory
US-0051 Done P1 As a developer, I can use `clear` to reset the terminal screen. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0139 `clear` resets terminal output
US-0052 Done P0 As a developer, I can run `node <file>` to execute JavaScript files. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0140 `node <file>` executes JS via WebView engine, capturing console.log output
  • ✓ AC-0141 `node <file>` with `require()` returns a clear error
US-0053 Done P0 As a developer, I can use `npm run <script>` to run package.json scripts. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0142 `npm run <script>` reads package.json and runs the matching script
  • ✓ AC-0143 `npm run <missing>` returns 'not found in package.json' error
  • ✓ AC-0144 `npm install` returns 'not supported' error
US-0054 Done P1 As a developer, I can use `npx prettier` offline to format code. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0145 `npx prettier <file>` formats the file using pre-bundled prettier
  • ✓ AC-0146 `npx <unknown>` returns 'not available' with list of bundled tools
US-0055 Done P0 As a developer, I see clear error messages for unsupported operations. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0147 All unsupported operations return specific, actionable error messages
US-0056 Done P0 App.tsx uses TerminalWebView for the live terminal experience. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0148 App.tsx renders TerminalWebView instead of the deprecated Terminal stub

  • ✓ AC-0047 CommandPalette renders all provided commands when the query is empty → TC-0137 Pass

EPIC-0005 Done Customization Release 1.0 (GA) $8,800 projected
US-0018 Done P1 As a developer, I want to switch between light and dark themes, so that I can use the app comfortably in any environment. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0054 App renders using Deep Slate (#0F172A) background in dark mode by default → TC-0149 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0055 A theme toggle switches the editor and UI surfaces to Off-White (#F9FAFB) background → TC-0150 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0056 All text/background pairs in both themes meet WCAG 4.5:1 contrast for normal text → TC-0151 Pass
US-0019 Done P1 As a developer, I want to change font size, so that I can read comfortably on my device. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0057 Pressing A+ in the editor toolbar increments the Monaco font size by 1pt → TC-0152 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0058 Pressing A- in the editor toolbar decrements the Monaco font size by 1pt → TC-0153 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0059 The current font size value is displayed in the toolbar between the A- and A+ buttons → TC-0154 Pass
US-0020 Done P1 As a power user, I want to install extensions, so that I can enhance the IDE with tools I need. XL · $3,200
  • ✓ AC-0060 An extension manifest can be registered in ExtensionRegistry and retrieved by id → TC-0156 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0061 activateExtension registers the manifest and returns valid sandbox HTML containing the extension source → TC-0158 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0062 deactivateExtension removes the extension from ExtensionRegistry → TC-0160 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0063 Extensions run in an isolated WebView sandbox — the sandbox HTML wraps source in try/catch → TC-0161 Pass
US-0057 Done P2 As a user, I want an About screen showing the splash and app info, so that I can identify the app version and credits. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0149 A ⓘ button in the status bar has a minimum 44pt touch target and is accessible via aria-label="About NomadCode"
  • ✓ AC-0150 Tapping ⓘ opens a full-screen modal displaying the splash image
  • ✓ AC-0151 The About modal footer shows the app version (from package.json) and copyright line
  • ✓ AC-0152 Tapping the ✕ close button dismisses the About modal with a fade animation
US-0067 Deferred P1 As a user, I want the Monaco editor's syntax colours to match my selected NomadCode theme, so that my chosen look (Dracula, Nord, Solarized Light, etc.) is reflected in code colours — not just the app chrome. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0202 Each NomadCode theme ID (nomad-dark, one-dark-pro, dracula, monokai, nord, tokyo-night, nomad-light, github-light, solarized-light, catppuccin-latte, night-owl-light) is registered with monaco.editor.defineTheme on editor boot with a full IStandaloneThemeData palette (colors + token rules)
  • ○ AC-0203 getMonacoTheme() returns the registered custom theme name (not vs / vs-dark) so custom colours are applied instead of the Monaco built-in fallback
  • ○ AC-0204 buildMonacoHtml() injects a defineTheme call for every registered theme before the initial setTheme, so the editor never shows a vs-dark flash on custom themes
  • ○ AC-0205 Switching themes in Settings or SetupWizard updates Monaco's active theme within one frame, with no unstyled interval
  • ○ AC-0206 Unit tests confirm each theme's defineTheme payload contains non-empty colors.background, colors.foreground, and at least one token rule for keywords, strings, and comments
US-0100 Planned P2 As a power user, I want to browse and install extensions directly from the VS Code Marketplace inside NomadCode, so that I can discover and add tools without leaving the IDE. ⚠ At Risk XL · $3,200
  • ○ AC-0303 An "Extensions" panel is accessible from the sidebar or command palette, displaying a searchable list of VS Code Marketplace extensions
  • ○ AC-0304 Each extension listing shows name, publisher, rating, download count, description, and a one-tap Install button
  • ○ AC-0305 Installing an extension downloads only the pure-JS / WASM portions of the extension package; any native binary components are silently omitted with an explanatory note (App Store §2.5.2 compliance — no runtime native code download)
  • ○ AC-0306 Installed extensions appear in the Extensions panel under "Installed" and are activated via the existing WASM-sandbox ExtensionHost
  • ○ AC-0307 Extension data (search results, extension metadata) is cached locally for offline browsing; install requires network

EPIC-0007 Done Authentication Release 1.0 (GA) $2,400 projected
US-0022 Done P0 As a developer, I want to sign in with my GitHub account, so that I can access private repositories and push code. L · $1,600
  • ✓ AC-0064 Tapping "Sign in with GitHub" launches the system browser with the correct OAuth authorize URL
  • ✓ AC-0065 After successful OAuth callback, an access token is returned and stored in the keychain
  • ✓ AC-0066 The user's GitHub username and avatar are displayed in the settings screen after sign-in
US-0023 Done P0 As a developer, I want my auth token stored securely, so that my credentials are never exposed. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0067 OAuth access token is stored in the platform keychain (iOS Keychain / Android Keystore), not in app storage or logs
  • ✓ AC-0068 Token survives app restart and is retrieved without re-authentication
US-0024 Done P1 As a developer, I want to sign out, so that I can switch accounts or revoke access. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0069 Tapping "Sign out" deletes the token from the keychain and clears the user session
  • ✓ AC-0070 After sign-out, git operations requiring auth prompt re-authentication
EPIC-0008 Done Git Integration Release 1.0 (GA) $4,800 projected
US-0025 Complete P0 As a developer, I want to clone a GitHub repository, so that I can work on an existing project. L · $1,600
  • ✓ AC-0071 User can enter a GitHub repository URL and initiate a clone to local storage → TC-0360 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0072 Clone progress is shown via a progress indicator; errors surface a readable message
  • ✓ AC-0073 Cloned repo appears in the file explorer immediately on completion
  • ✓ AC-0191 If the repository requires authentication and no GitHub token is stored, the user sees a clear message and a path to sign in (e.g. Settings)
  • ✓ AC-0192 When the network is unavailable or clone fails transiently, the user sees an actionable message; transient failures retry with exponential backoff up to three times (per AGENTS.md) → TC-0362 Pass
US-0026 Complete P0 As a developer, I want to stage and commit changes, so that I can record my work in git history. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0074 Modified files are listed in the git status panel with staged/unstaged indicators
  • ✓ AC-0075 Tapping a file checkbox toggles it between staged and unstaged
  • ✓ AC-0076 Entering a commit message and confirming creates a local commit
  • ✓ AC-0193 Git status panel interactive controls have accessibility labels and minimum 44pt touch targets (WCAG-minded)
US-0027 Complete P0 As a developer, I want to push and pull from remote, so that I can collaborate with my team. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0077 Tapping Push sends local commits to the remote using the stored OAuth token
  • ✓ AC-0078 Tapping Pull fetches and merges remote changes into the working directory
  • ✓ AC-0194 If push or pull requires authentication and no GitHub token is stored, the user sees a clear message and a path to sign in (e.g. Settings)
  • ✓ AC-0195 When offline or push/pull fails transiently, the user sees an actionable message; transient failures retry with exponential backoff up to three times (per AGENTS.md)
US-0028 Complete P1 As a developer, I want to view file diffs, so that I can review changes before committing. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0079 Tapping a modified file in the git status panel shows an inline diff view
  • ✓ AC-0080 Added lines are highlighted green; removed lines are highlighted red
US-0029 Complete P1 As a developer, I want to create and switch branches, so that I can work on features in isolation. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0081 Current branch name is visible in the status bar
  • ✓ AC-0082 User can create a new branch from the current HEAD via the branch panel
  • ✓ AC-0083 User can switch to an existing local branch without losing uncommitted changes
EPIC-0009 Planned In-App Purchases & Monetization Release 1.0 (GA) $2,800 projected
US-0030 Planned P1 As a free-tier user, I want clear limits displayed, so that I understand what the free plan includes. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0084 Free tier users can open up to 3 files simultaneously; attempting a 4th shows an upgrade prompt
  • ○ AC-0085 Free tier label is displayed in the settings screen alongside an upgrade CTA
US-0031 Planned P0 As a developer, I want to subscribe to Pro, so that I can unlock unlimited files and advanced features. ⚠ At Risk L · $1,600
  • ○ AC-0086 Pro subscription is purchasable via the native IAP sheet on iOS and Google Play Billing on Android
  • ○ AC-0087 Successful purchase removes file-count limits and unlocks Pro-gated features immediately
  • ○ AC-0088 Subscription status persists across app restarts via server-side receipt validation
US-0032 Planned P0 As a power user, I want to subscribe to Pro+AI, so that I can use AI-powered code suggestions. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0089 Pro+AI subscription unlocks AI Suggestions features in addition to all Pro features
  • ○ AC-0090 Purchasing Pro+AI when already on Pro upgrades the subscription without double-charging
EPIC-0010 Planned AI Suggestions Release 1.0 (GA) $5,200 projected
US-0033 Planned P0 As a Pro+AI user, I want inline code completions, so that I can write code faster. ⚠ At Risk XL · $3,200
  • ○ AC-0091 While typing, ghost-text completions appear after a 300 ms debounce
  • ○ AC-0092 Pressing Tab accepts the current completion; pressing Escape dismisses it
  • ○ AC-0093 Completions are only triggered for Pro+AI subscribers; other users see no ghost text
US-0034 Planned P0 As a Pro+AI user, I want an AI chat panel, so that I can ask questions about my code. ⚠ At Risk L · $1,600
  • ○ AC-0094 A chat panel can be opened alongside the editor via a toolbar button
  • ○ AC-0095 User can type a prompt referencing the current file and receive a streamed response
  • ○ AC-0096 Chat history persists for the session and is cleared on app restart
US-0035 Planned P1 As a non-subscriber, I want AI features gated with a clear upgrade prompt, so that I understand what I'm missing. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0097 Non-Pro+AI users see a paywall prompt when attempting to open the AI chat panel
  • ○ AC-0098 AI completion ghost text is hidden entirely for Free and Pro tier users
EPIC-0011 Planned App Store & EAS Build Delivery Release 1.0 (GA) $3,600 projected
US-0036 Planned P0 As a release engineer, I want an EAS Build pipeline, so that I can produce store-ready binaries from CI. ⚠ At Risk L · $1,600
  • ○ AC-0099 `eas build --platform all` produces a valid iOS .ipa and Android .aab without errors
  • ○ AC-0100 Build configuration is defined in `eas.json` with a production profile
US-0058 Done P0 As a release engineer, I want branded app icons, splash screens, and named APK artefacts, so that the app presents a polished identity in stores and on device home screens. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0153 App icon (1024×1024 NomadCode logo) is correctly referenced in `app.json`, `ios/Images.xcassets/AppIcon`, and all Android mipmap densities (mdpi–xxxhdpi WebP)
  • ✓ AC-0154 Splash screen displays the NomadCode logo centred on Deep Slate (#0F172A) background; `app.json` splash config uses `resizeMode: "contain"` and matching backgroundColor
  • ✓ AC-0155 Android APK build artefacts are named `NomadCode-debug.apk` and `NomadCode-release.apk` via `applicationVariants.all` in `android/app/build.gradle`
US-0037 Planned P0 As a release engineer, I want to submit to the iOS App Store, so that users can install on iPhone and iPad. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0101 `eas submit --platform ios` successfully uploads the build to App Store Connect
  • ○ AC-0102 App metadata (name, description, screenshots, privacy policy URL) is complete in App Store Connect
US-0038 Planned P0 As a release engineer, I want to submit to Google Play, so that users can install on Android devices. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0103 `eas submit --platform android` successfully uploads the build to the Google Play Console
  • ○ AC-0104 App metadata (title, description, screenshots) is complete in the Play Console internal track
EPIC-0012 Done Cloud Sync Release 1.1 (Post-Launch) $3,200 projected
US-0039 Done P1 As a developer, I want to upload my project to cloud storage, so that I can access it from multiple devices. L · $1,600
  • ✓ AC-0105 User can connect an S3-compatible bucket by entering endpoint URL and access credentials in settings
  • ✓ AC-0106 Modified local files are uploaded to the configured bucket automatically on save
US-0040 Done P1 As a developer, I want to download remote changes on launch, so that my files are always up to date. L · $1,600
  • ✓ AC-0107 On app launch with network access, remote changes are pulled and merged into local storage
  • ✓ AC-0108 Sync conflicts are surfaced to the user with a merge prompt before overwriting local files
EPIC-0013 Done Multi-Language Editor Support Release 1.1 (Post-Launch) $2,400 projected
US-0041 Done P1 As a developer, I want syntax highlighting for Python, Rust, Go, and Swift, so that I can work in my preferred language. M · $800.00
  • ✓ AC-0109 Python, Rust, Go, and Swift files receive correct syntax highlighting
  • ✓ AC-0110 Language is auto-detected from file extension; unknown extensions fall back to plain text
US-0042 Done P2 As a developer, I want language-correct indentation and auto-close, so that the editor follows language conventions. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0111 Python files default to 4-space indentation; Go files default to tabs
  • ✓ AC-0112 Bracket and quote auto-close is active for all supported languages
US-0059 Done P0 As a developer, I want the editor to apply language-appropriate indentation settings automatically when I open a file, so that code I type is formatted consistently with the conventions of that language. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0156 Python files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=true, detectIndentation=false
  • ✓ AC-0157 Go files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=false (hard tabs per gofmt)
  • ✓ AC-0158 Rust and Swift files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=true
  • ✓ AC-0159 C, C++, C#, F#, Java, and Kotlin files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=true
  • ✓ AC-0160 TypeScript and JavaScript files use tabSize=2, insertSpaces=true
  • ✓ AC-0161 Dart, HTML, CSS, JSON, YAML, and Markdown files use tabSize=2, insertSpaces=true
  • ✓ AC-0162 detectIndentation is always false — Monaco must not override configured rules
  • ✓ AC-0163 When switching tabs, indentation rules for the new tab's language are applied within the same message cycle as the content load
US-0060 Done P1 As a developer, I want language-specific auto-close bracket behaviour, so that bracket and quote completion matches the language I am writing. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0164 Python files use autoClosingQuotes='languageDefined' (defers to Monaco's built-in Python grammar to avoid doubling quotes inside strings)
  • ✓ AC-0165 Ruby, R, HTML, and XML files use autoClosingQuotes='languageDefined'
  • ✓ AC-0166 All other supported languages use autoClosingBrackets='always' and autoClosingQuotes='always'
  • ✓ AC-0167 Switching from a Python tab to a Go tab updates autoClosingQuotes from 'languageDefined' to 'always'
US-0061 Done P1 As a developer working with Zig, Dart, Lua, Elixir, C#, F#, R, Scala, Perl, PowerShell, Terraform, or Protocol Buffers files, I want NomadCode to recognise those extensions and apply a Monaco language mode and correct indentation. S · $400.00
  • ✓ AC-0168 .cs → csharp, .fs/.fsx → fsharp, .zig → zig, .dart → dart, .lua → lua, .ex/.exs → elixir, .r/.R → r, .scala → scala, .pl/.pm → perl, .ps1/.psm1 → powershell, .tf/.hcl → hcl, .proto → proto, .ini/.cfg/.env → ini
  • ✓ AC-0169 Extension matching is case-insensitive (e.g. App.DART → dart)
  • ✓ AC-0170 Vue SFCs (.vue) fall back to html language mode (no dedicated Monaco grammar)
  • ✓ AC-0171 jsonc files map to Monaco language ID 'jsonc'; toml files map to 'toml'
EPIC-0014 Done Global Search — Find in Files Release 1.1 (Post-Launch) $2,000 projected

  • ○ AC-0113 A global search panel can be opened via the command palette or a dedicated toolbar button → TC-0355 Pass
  • ○ AC-0114 Entering a query returns all matching lines across the project file tree → TC-0347 Pass
  • ○ AC-0115 Results are grouped by file with file path, line number, and line preview shown → TC-0352 Pass
US-0044 Done P1 As a developer, I want to navigate to a search result, so that I can jump directly to the relevant code. S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0116 Tapping a search result opens the file and scrolls to the matching line → TC-0357 Pass
  • ○ AC-0117 The matching text is highlighted in the editor after navigation → TC-0359 Pass
EPIC-0015 Deferred Crash Reporting & Observability Release 1.1 (Post-Launch) $1,600 projected
US-0045 Deferred P0 As a developer, I want crashes reported automatically, so that I can fix production issues quickly. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0118 Unhandled exceptions and crashes are automatically reported to the configured crash reporting service
  • ○ AC-0119 No PII, user file content, or credentials are included in crash payloads
US-0046 Deferred P1 As a developer, I want performance metrics tracked, so that I can detect regressions before users do. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0120 Cold start time, editor input latency, and memory usage are tracked as named metrics
  • ○ AC-0121 Performance degradation beyond baseline thresholds (§ 8 of PROJECT.md) triggers an alert
EPIC-0019 Deferred Native iOS Folder Picker Release 1.2 (Post-Launch) $1,600 projected
US-0066 Deferred P1 As an iOS user, I want to pick a workspace folder from iCloud Drive, On My iPad, or any File Provider (Dropbox, OneDrive, etc.), so that my NomadCode workspace lives where my other files are and syncs across devices. ⚠ At Risk L · $1,600
  • ○ AC-0196 Custom native iOS module (Swift) wraps UIDocumentPickerViewController in directory-selection mode (.folder UTType on iOS 14+)
  • ○ AC-0197 Selected folder URL is converted to a security-scoped URL bookmark (NSURL bookmarkData) and persisted so write access survives app relaunches
  • ○ AC-0198 Before each git write, the module calls startAccessingSecurityScopedResource() and pairs it with stopAccessingSecurityScopedResource() after completion to avoid leaking entitlements
  • ○ AC-0199 SetupWizard and Settings use the new picker via requestWorkspacePermission() — existing call site unchanged
  • ○ AC-0200 When the picker is unavailable (e.g. user cancels, iOS < 14), the in-app SandboxDirectoryPicker is offered as fallback
  • ○ AC-0201 Unit + E2E tests confirm clone/commit/push works in an iCloud Drive folder on a physical device
EPIC-0020 Planned Advanced Git Workflows Release 1.0 (GA) $4,000 projected
US-0068 Planned P0 As a developer, I want to create and switch branches from the mobile UI, so that I can manage feature work without needing a desktop. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0207 The Git panel exposes a "New Branch" action that accepts a name and creates the branch locally via isomorphic-git
  • ○ AC-0208 A branch picker (bottom sheet on phone, sidebar section on tablet) lists local and remote branches and switches HEAD on tap
  • ○ AC-0209 The active branch name is shown in the status bar and updates immediately after a switch without requiring an app restart
US-0069 Planned P0 As a developer, I want a merge conflict resolution UI, so that I can resolve conflicts without leaving the app. ⚠ At Risk L · $1,600
  • ○ AC-0210 Files with merge conflicts are marked with a conflict badge in the File Explorer; tapping opens a 3-panel diff view (ours / base / theirs)
  • ○ AC-0211 Each conflicted hunk has Accept Ours, Accept Theirs, and Accept Both buttons; accepting a choice writes the resolved content to the file
  • ○ AC-0212 Once all conflicts are resolved, a "Mark Resolved & Stage" action is available; the conflict badge clears and the file is staged automatically
US-0070 Planned P1 As a developer, I want git gutter indicators in the editor, so that I can see which lines have been added, modified, or deleted since the last commit. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0213 Lines added since HEAD are marked with a green bar in the Monaco gutter; modified lines show amber; deleted positions show a red triangle
  • ○ AC-0214 Gutter indicators update within 500 ms of saving the file (diff is computed against the HEAD version in isomorphic-git's object store)
  • ○ AC-0215 Tapping a gutter indicator opens a compact inline diff popup showing the original line(s) with a Revert Hunk action
US-0071 Planned P1 As a developer, I want to stash and pop changes, so that I can context-switch without losing work in progress. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0216 The Git panel has a "Stash Changes" action that stashes all unstaged modifications with an optional message
  • ○ AC-0217 A stash list shows all named stashes; tapping a stash offers Pop (apply + drop) and Apply (apply, keep stash) actions
  • ○ AC-0218 Popping or applying a stash updates the working tree and refreshes gutter indicators without requiring a manual refresh
US-0072 Planned P2 As a developer, I want to see git blame annotations, so that I can understand the history of each line of code. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0219 A "Toggle Blame" action in the editor toolbar overlays each line with the commit hash, author, and relative timestamp
  • ○ AC-0220 Tapping a blame annotation opens a detail sheet with the full commit message, author, date, and a "View Diff" link that opens the commit diff
  • ○ AC-0221 Blame data is loaded asynchronously; a loading skeleton is shown while git log is computed so the editor remains responsive
EPIC-0021 Planned Advanced Editor Features Release 1.1 (Post-Launch) $3,600 projected
US-0073 Planned P0 As a developer, I want to search and replace across all files in my project, so that I can do global refactors without a desktop IDE. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0222 The Global Search panel (EPIC-0014) gains a Replace field; entering a replacement term and pressing Replace All writes changes to every matching file simultaneously
  • ○ AC-0223 A preview mode shows all replacements highlighted before confirmation; the developer can exclude individual matches with a checkbox
  • ○ AC-0224 Replace supports regex with capture groups (e.g. $1); the preview updates in real time as the pattern changes
US-0074 Planned P0 As a developer using a hardware keyboard, I want standard shortcuts like ⌘S, ⌘`, ⌘N, and ⌘P, so that I can edit at desktop speed on a tablet. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0225 ⌘S saves the current file immediately; ⌘⇧S saves all open dirty files
  • ○ AC-0226 ⌘` opens/focuses the integrated terminal; pressing again cycles between terminal and editor
  • ○ AC-0227 ⌘N creates a new untitled file in the editor; ⌘P opens the Command Palette; all shortcuts are discoverable in a Keyboard Shortcuts help sheet
US-0075 Planned P1 As a developer, I want to fold and unfold code blocks, so that I can reduce visual noise when navigating large files. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0228 Monaco gutter displays fold/unfold chevrons for all foldable regions (functions, classes, blocks, comments)
  • ○ AC-0229 A "Fold All" and "Unfold All" action is available in the editor context menu and Command Palette
  • ○ AC-0230 Folded state persists per file within a session; reopening a tab restores the previous fold state
US-0076 Planned P1 As a developer, I want my code auto-formatted on save, so that I don't have to think about style consistency. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0231 Prettier (bundled as a WASM module) runs on file save for JS, TS, JSON, CSS, HTML, and Markdown files
  • ○ AC-0232 Format on save is toggleable in Settings; when disabled, a "Format Document" action is still available in the Command Palette
  • ○ AC-0233 Prettier config is read from the project root (.prettierrc, prettier.config.js) if present; defaults apply otherwise
US-0077 Planned P2 As a developer, I want breadcrumb navigation at the top of the editor, so that I can quickly understand and jump to any scope in a large file. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0234 A breadcrumb bar above the editor shows the file path segments and the current symbol hierarchy (e.g. File › ClassName › methodName)
  • ○ AC-0235 Tapping any breadcrumb segment opens a picker listing sibling symbols at that depth; selecting one navigates the cursor to that symbol
  • ○ AC-0236 Breadcrumbs update within one animation frame of moving the cursor to a new symbol
US-0078 Planned P2 As a developer, I want snippet expansion, so that I can scaffold common patterns with a short prefix. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0237 Built-in snippets for JS/TS (e.g. `clg` → console.log, `afn` → arrow function), Python (`def`, `cls`), and React (`rfc`, `useEffect`) ship with the app
  • ○ AC-0238 Users can define custom snippets in Settings using a JSON schema; snippets support tab stops ($1, $2) and default values ($\{1:name\})
  • ○ AC-0239 Snippets appear in Monaco's autocomplete list with a snippet icon; Tab accepts the first suggestion matching the typed prefix
EPIC-0022 Planned Code Navigation Release 1.1 (Post-Launch) $2,000 projected
US-0079 Planned P0 As a developer, I want Go to Definition, so that I can jump to where a symbol is declared without manual searching. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0240 Long-pressing a symbol opens a context menu with "Go to Definition"; tapping navigates the editor to the declaration line, opening the file if necessary
  • ○ AC-0241 ⌘Click (hardware keyboard) also triggers Go to Definition inline with VS Code muscle memory
  • ○ AC-0242 If the definition is in an external node_modules package, a read-only preview of the declaration is shown in an overlay panel rather than opening the file
US-0080 Planned P0 As a developer, I want Find All References, so that I can see every place a symbol is used before renaming or deleting it. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0243 "Find All References" in the symbol context menu opens a References panel listing every usage grouped by file with line previews
  • ○ AC-0244 Tapping any result navigates to that line in the editor; the panel stays open so the developer can step through each reference
  • ○ AC-0245 Result count is shown in the panel header; empty results display a "No references found" state rather than crashing or silently closing
US-0081 Planned P1 As a developer, I want workspace symbol search, so that I can jump to any function or class by name without knowing which file it's in. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0246 The Command Palette gains a "Go to Symbol in Workspace" action (⌘T shortcut on hardware keyboard) that fuzzy-searches all exported symbols across all project files
  • ○ AC-0247 Results show symbol name, type (function/class/variable), and file path; selecting one navigates directly to the declaration
  • ○ AC-0248 Symbol index is built lazily on first use and refreshed on file save; an incremental spinner indicates a background refresh without blocking the UI
EPIC-0023 Planned AI Code Intelligence Release 1.1 (Post-Launch) $3,200 projected
US-0082 Planned P0 As a developer, I want to select code and describe a change in plain language, so that I can make AI-driven edits without leaving the editor. ⚠ At Risk L · $1,600
  • ○ AC-0249 Selecting a code range and invoking "AI Edit" (⌘K on hardware keyboard or long-press context menu) opens an inline prompt bar above the selection
  • ○ AC-0250 After the developer types an instruction and submits, the AI returns a diff which is applied in a preview mode — Accept and Reject buttons confirm or discard
  • ○ AC-0251 The inline AI edit is only available to Pro+AI subscribers; Free and Pro users see an upgrade prompt when invoking the action
US-0083 Planned P1 As a developer, I want to define project-scoped AI instructions, so that AI suggestions follow my team's conventions without me repeating them every session. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0252 A `.nomadcode/ai-rules.md` file in the project root is automatically loaded as system context for all AI requests in that project
  • ○ AC-0253 The Settings screen exposes a global "AI Rules" editor for rules that apply across all projects; project-level rules override global rules where they conflict
  • ○ AC-0254 AI rules are never sent to the AI provider if the session has privacy mode enabled (US-0091); a banner informs the user that rules are suppressed
US-0084 Planned P1 As a developer, I want AI-generated commit messages, so that I can write better commit history with less effort. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0255 The Git Commit panel has a "Generate Message" button that sends the staged diff to the AI and populates the message field with a conventional-commit formatted summary
  • ○ AC-0256 The developer can regenerate or manually edit the suggestion before committing; the generated text is never committed automatically
  • ○ AC-0257 The feature is gated behind Pro+AI; Free/Pro users see a disabled button with an upgrade tooltip
US-0085 Planned P1 As a power user, I want to choose which AI model powers my completions and chat, so that I am not locked into a single provider. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0258 Settings exposes a Model Picker with options for Claude (default), GPT-4o, and Gemini 1.5 Pro; selecting one updates all subsequent AI requests
  • ○ AC-0259 Each provider requires the user to enter their own API key; keys are stored in the platform keychain (iOS Keychain / Android Keystore)
  • ○ AC-0260 If the selected provider returns an authentication error, a non-blocking banner prompts the user to re-enter their key; the previous working provider is used as fallback
EPIC-0024 Planned Language Server Protocol Release 1.2 (Post-Launch) $5,600 projected
US-0086 Planned P0 As a developer, I want a TypeScript language server running in a WASM worker, so that I get accurate completions and type information without a server round-trip. ⚠ At Risk XL · $3,200
  • ○ AC-0261 TypeScript language server (typescript-language-server compiled to WASM) initialises in a Web Worker within 3 s of opening the first .ts or .tsx file
  • ○ AC-0262 The LSP worker communicates with the Monaco editor via the Language Client protocol; completions, hover docs, and go-to-definition results are served from the worker
  • ○ AC-0263 The WASM bundle is shipped with the app (not downloaded at runtime) to comply with App Store §2.5.2 and Google Play equivalent
US-0087 Planned P0 As a developer, I want IntelliSense completions, so that I get context-aware suggestions including imported symbols, methods, and types. ⚠ At Risk L · $1,600
  • ○ AC-0264 Typing in a .ts file triggers LSP-powered completions within 200 ms; suggestions include local variables, imported symbols, and type members
  • ○ AC-0265 Hovering (or long-pressing on touch) a symbol shows a hover card with the TypeScript type signature and JSDoc documentation if available
  • ○ AC-0266 Completions rank exact-match prefixes highest; accepted completions auto-import the module if not already imported
US-0088 Planned P0 As a developer, I want real-time error and warning squiggles, so that I can see type errors without running the compiler. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0267 TypeScript diagnostics from the LSP worker are rendered as red (error) or amber (warning) squiggles under the relevant tokens in the editor
  • ○ AC-0268 Tapping a squiggle opens an inline tooltip with the error message, error code, and a "Quick Fix" button when an LSP code action is available
  • ○ AC-0269 A Problems panel (accessible from the status bar) lists all errors and warnings across open files; tapping an entry navigates to the offending line
EPIC-0025 Planned AI Codebase Indexing Release 1.2 (Post-Launch) $5,200 projected
US-0089 Planned P0 As a developer, I want my project indexed semantically, so that the AI understands the full codebase — not just the current file. ⚠ At Risk XL · $3,200
  • ○ AC-0270 On first open of a project, a background indexer computes vector embeddings for all source files (JS, TS, Python, Go, Swift, Kotlin) using a bundled embedding model
  • ○ AC-0271 The index is stored on-device in SQLite (using the sqlite-vec extension); incremental re-indexing runs on file save, touching only changed files
  • ○ AC-0272 A status bar indicator shows indexing progress; the AI features degrade gracefully (file-only context) until indexing completes
US-0090 Planned P0 As a developer, I want to reference @file and @codebase in AI chat, so that the AI can answer questions about specific files or the whole project. ⚠ At Risk L · $1,600
  • ○ AC-0273 Typing @ in the AI chat panel opens a completion picker showing project files; selecting one appends its content (up to 8 k tokens) to the AI context
  • ○ AC-0274 @codebase triggers a semantic search of the index and appends the top-5 most relevant chunks to the AI context automatically
  • ○ AC-0275 Token budget for attached context is displayed in the chat input bar; exceeding the limit truncates the oldest attachment first and shows a warning
US-0091 Planned P1 As a developer working with sensitive code, I want a privacy mode that disables codebase indexing and AI context upload, so that proprietary code never leaves my device. ⚠ At Risk S · $400.00
  • ○ AC-0276 A per-project Privacy Mode toggle in Settings disables all indexing and suppresses any code context from being sent to external AI providers
  • ○ AC-0277 When Privacy Mode is on, AI completions and chat fall back to prompt-only mode; a persistent banner reminds the user that context is limited
  • ○ AC-0278 Privacy Mode state is stored locally and is never synced to cloud storage; it defaults to off for new projects
EPIC-0026 Planned Mobile Debugging Release 1.2 (Post-Launch) $5,600 projected
US-0092 Planned P0 As a developer, I want to set breakpoints in the editor, so that I can pause execution and inspect state at any line. ⚠ At Risk XL · $3,200
  • ○ AC-0279 Tapping the Monaco gutter toggles a breakpoint indicator (red dot); the breakpoint is registered with the WASI debug adapter
  • ○ AC-0280 When execution hits a breakpoint, the editor scrolls to the paused line and highlights it; a debug toolbar (Continue, Step Over, Step Into, Step Out) appears
  • ○ AC-0281 Breakpoints persist across sessions in a per-project SQLite store; they are automatically removed if the file is deleted
US-0093 Planned P1 As a developer, I want a Watch panel, so that I can monitor specific expressions as I step through code. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0282 The Debug sidebar has a Watch panel where the developer can add any expression; expressions are re-evaluated at each breakpoint pause
  • ○ AC-0283 Watch expressions that resolve to objects display an expandable tree; primitive values show inline
  • ○ AC-0284 Adding a watch expression while not paused shows a "Waiting for breakpoint" placeholder; it evaluates automatically at the next pause
US-0094 Planned P1 As a developer, I want a call stack panel, so that I can understand how execution reached the current breakpoint. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0285 When paused, the Call Stack panel lists all active frames with function name, file, and line number
  • ○ AC-0286 Tapping a frame navigates the editor to that line and updates the Watch and Variables panels to reflect that frame's scope
  • ○ AC-0287 Async frames are visually distinguished from synchronous frames with an italic label and a different colour
US-0095 Planned P1 As a developer, I want a debug console, so that I can evaluate expressions and see console output while paused. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0288 The Debug Console panel streams console.log / print / fmt.Println output from the running process in real time
  • ○ AC-0289 An input bar at the bottom accepts REPL-style expressions; results are printed inline in the console stream
  • ○ AC-0290 Console output is colour-coded: stdout in default text, stderr in coral (#EF4444), and evaluated results in teal (#0D9488)
EPIC-0027 Planned Collaboration & Sharing Release 1.2 (Post-Launch) $4,800 projected
US-0096 Planned P1 As a developer using multiple devices, I want my settings synced, so that my theme, keybindings, and AI rules are consistent everywhere. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0291 Editor settings (theme, font size, keybindings, AI rules) are serialised to JSON and synced to the user's cloud storage bucket on change (EPIC-0012)
  • ○ AC-0292 On app launch, the latest settings JSON is fetched and applied before the editor renders; a conflict (two devices edited at the same time) shows a merge diff
  • ○ AC-0293 Settings sync is opt-in and off by default; a Settings screen toggle enables it and shows the last sync timestamp
US-0097 Planned P2 As a developer, I want to share a code snippet as a public link, so that I can quickly show colleagues a piece of code without setting up a gist manually. ⚠ At Risk M · $800.00
  • ○ AC-0294 Selecting a code range and choosing "Share Snippet" generates a short URL pointing to a syntax-highlighted, read-only web page hosted by NomadCode's share service
  • ○ AC-0295 The share URL is valid for 30 days and includes the language, filename, and highlighted range; the developer can extend or revoke it from Settings › Shared Snippets
  • ○ AC-0296 Shared snippets contain no auth tokens or personal data beyond the code content; the share service stores only the snippet and expiry timestamp
US-0098 Planned P2 As a developer, I want to invite a collaborator to view my session in real time, so that we can pair-program without screen sharing overhead. ⚠ At Risk XL · $3,200
  • ○ AC-0297 The host generates a session invite link from the Collaboration menu; the collaborator opens it in NomadCode (or browser) and sees the editor state in real time (read-only by default)
  • ○ AC-0298 The host can grant edit access to one collaborator at a time; cursors are shown with name labels in distinct colours; concurrent edits are resolved with operational transforms
  • ○ AC-0299 The session is end-to-end encrypted (WebRTC DTLS); no code is stored on NomadCode servers — the relay only forwards encrypted packets between peers
EPIC-0001 Done Code Editing MVP (v0.1) $4,800 projected
Done P0 US-0001

As a developer, I want to open a file on my device, so that I can read and edit existing code.

M $800.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0009 FileExplorer displays the device file tree in the sidebar → TC-0081 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0010 Tapping a file loads its content into the editor → TC-0084 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0011 Active file path is displayed in the editor path bar → TC-0086 Pass
Done P0 US-0002

As a developer, I want to edit code with syntax highlighting, so that I can read code more easily.

L $1,600 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0012 TypeScript/JavaScript files receive correct syntax highlighting (language detected from extension) → TC-0089 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0013 Editor is responsive to touch input — tap positions cursor, pinch adjusts font size → TC-0092 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0014 Virtual keyboard raises the editor viewport so the cursor line remains visible → TC-0094 Pass
Done P0 US-0003

As a developer, I want to save my changes, so that my work is not lost.

S $400.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0022 Cmd+S / toolbar Save triggers onSave with the active file path and current content → TC-0096 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0023 After save, the dirty indicator (●) is removed from the active tab → TC-0098 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0024 Saving a file with no changes (clean tab) calls onSave without error → TC-0100 Pass
Done P0 US-0004

As a developer, I want to undo and redo changes, so that I can recover from mistakes.

S $400.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0025 Pressing the Undo toolbar button sends the undo command to Monaco without throwing → TC-0101 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0026 Pressing the Redo toolbar button sends the redo command to Monaco without throwing → TC-0103 Pass
Done P1 US-0005

As a developer, I want to search and replace text within a file, so that I can refactor code quickly.

M $800.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0027 Pressing the Find toolbar button sends the find command to Monaco without throwing → TC-0104 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0028 Find toolbar button is accessible via aria-label and has a minimum 44pt touch target → TC-0105 Pass
Done P1 US-0006

As a developer, I want to view multiple files in tabs, so that I can work across files without losing context.

M $800.00 4/4 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0029 Tab bar renders one tab per open file with correct label → TC-0106 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0030 Tapping a tab calls onTabChange with that file's path → TC-0109 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0031 Pressing the × close button calls onTabClose with the correct path → TC-0110 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0032 Dirty tabs show ● prefix; clean tabs show no indicator → TC-0112 Pass
EPIC-0002 Done File Management MVP (v0.1) $4,000 projected
Done P0 US-0007

As a developer, I want to browse my project directory tree, so that I can find and open any file.

M $800.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0033 FileExplorer renders all file and directory entries returned by FileSystemBridge → TC-0114 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0034 Tapping a directory expands it and shows its children; tapping again collapses it → TC-0116 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0035 Long-pressing any entry opens a context menu with New File, New Folder, Rename, Move to, and Delete options → TC-0035 Pass
Done P0 US-0008

As a developer, I want to create new files and folders, so that I can add to my project.

S $400.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0015 New file created at correct path + tree refreshed (TC-0041..TC-0048) → TC-0041 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0016 New folder created at correct path + tree refreshed (TC-0049..TC-0052) → TC-0049 Pass
Done P0 US-0009

As a developer, I want to rename files and folders, so that I can keep my project organized.

S $400.00 1/1 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0017 Rename via moveEntry + tree refreshed; modal pre-filled with current name (TC-0053..TC-0058) → TC-0053 Pass
Done P0 US-0010

As a developer, I want to delete files and folders, so that I can clean up my project.

S $400.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0018 Confirmed delete calls deleteEntry + tree refreshed (TC-0059..TC-0065) → TC-0059 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0019 Cancel skips deleteEntry — no tree change (TC-0061) → TC-0060 Pass
Done P2 US-0011

As a developer, I want to move files and folders, so that I can restructure my project intuitively.

L $1,600 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0020 Move to valid destination calls moveEntry + onFileMove + tree reloaded (TC-0066..TC-0077) → TC-0066 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0021 Self-move and descendant-move blocked with error alert (TC-0073..TC-0074) → TC-0073 Pass
Done P1 US-0099

As a developer, I want file type icons in the Explorer sidebar, so that I can distinguish file types at a glance without reading every filename.

S $400.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0300 File entries display a coloured monospace badge (TSX, TS, JS, PY, MD, JSON, etc.) derived from the file extension, replacing the undifferentiated '·' bullet
  • ✓ AC-0301 Special filenames (.gitignore, package.json, Dockerfile, README.md, .env, etc.) receive distinct contextual badge labels (GIT, PKG, DOCK, DOC, ENV) regardless of extension
  • ✓ AC-0302 Directory entries retain the ▸/▾ chevron toggle icon and are visually distinct from file entries at all times
EPIC-0003 Done Terminal Release 0.5 (Beta) $8,800 projected
Done P0 US-0012

As a developer, I want to open an integrated terminal, so that I can run commands without leaving the app.

XL $3,200 4/4 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0036 Terminal component renders the TERMINAL header and a prompt character ($) on mount → TC-0121 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0037 Terminal accepts a workingDirectory prop and displays it in the welcome output → TC-0123 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0038 Terminal input clears after a command is submitted → TC-0125 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0039 Virtual keyboard raises the terminal viewport on iOS via KeyboardAvoidingView → TC-0126 Pass
Done P0 US-0013

As a developer, I want to run build and test commands, so that I can verify my code works.

M $800.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0040 Submitting a command echoes it in the output prefixed with $ → TC-0127 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0041 The onCommand callback is invoked with the exact command string on submit → TC-0129 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0042 Submitting an empty string does not add a line or call onCommand → TC-0130 Pass
Done P1 US-0014

As a developer, I want to see colored terminal output, so that I can parse logs more easily.

S $400.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0043 Command echo lines are rendered in a distinct color (green/success style) → TC-0132 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0044 Error/unknown-command lines are rendered in the error color (Coral #EF4444) → TC-0133 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0045 Regular output lines are rendered in the standard text color → TC-0134 Pass
Done P0 US-0050

As a developer, I can use `touch`, `cp`, and `mv` commands to create and manage files.

M $800.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0136 `touch <file>` creates a file if it doesn't exist; no-op if it does
  • ✓ AC-0137 `cp <src> <dest>` copies a file/directory
  • ✓ AC-0138 `mv <src> <dest>` moves/renames a file/directory
Done P1 US-0051

As a developer, I can use `clear` to reset the terminal screen.

S $400.00 1/1 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0139 `clear` resets terminal output
Done P0 US-0052

As a developer, I can run `node <file>` to execute JavaScript files.

M $800.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0140 `node <file>` executes JS via WebView engine, capturing console.log output
  • ✓ AC-0141 `node <file>` with `require()` returns a clear error
Done P0 US-0053

As a developer, I can use `npm run <script>` to run package.json scripts.

M $800.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0142 `npm run <script>` reads package.json and runs the matching script
  • ✓ AC-0143 `npm run <missing>` returns 'not found in package.json' error
  • ✓ AC-0144 `npm install` returns 'not supported' error
Done P1 US-0054

As a developer, I can use `npx prettier` offline to format code.

M $800.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0145 `npx prettier <file>` formats the file using pre-bundled prettier
  • ✓ AC-0146 `npx <unknown>` returns 'not available' with list of bundled tools
Done P0 US-0055

As a developer, I see clear error messages for unsupported operations.

S $400.00 1/1 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0147 All unsupported operations return specific, actionable error messages
Done P0 US-0056

App.tsx uses TerminalWebView for the live terminal experience.

S $400.00 1/1 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0148 App.tsx renders TerminalWebView instead of the deprecated Terminal stub
EPIC-0004 Done Command Palette Release 0.5 (Beta) $1,600 projected
Done P1 US-0015

As a power user, I want to open a command palette, so that I can run any action without navigating menus.

M $800.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0046 CommandPalette renders a search input that auto-focuses on mount → TC-0136 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0047 CommandPalette renders all provided commands when the query is empty → TC-0137 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0048 Tapping outside the panel (backdrop) dismisses the keyboard → TC-0139 Pass
Done P1 US-0016

As a power user, I want to search commands by name, so that I can find actions without memorizing locations.

S $400.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0049 Typing a query filters commands to those matching label or description (case-insensitive) → TC-0140 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0050 When no commands match the query, a "No commands found" message is shown → TC-0143 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0051 Pressing Enter selects the first result in the filtered list → TC-0144 Pass
Done P2 US-0017

As a power user, I want to see keyboard shortcuts in the palette, so that I can learn shortcuts over time.

S $400.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0052 Commands with a shortcut field display the shortcut in a badge alongside the label → TC-0146 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0053 Commands without a shortcut field show no badge (no empty badge rendered) → TC-0148 Pass
EPIC-0005 Done Customization Release 1.0 (GA) $8,800 projected
Done P1 US-0018

As a developer, I want to switch between light and dark themes, so that I can use the app comfortably in any environment.

M $800.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0054 App renders using Deep Slate (#0F172A) background in dark mode by default → TC-0149 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0055 A theme toggle switches the editor and UI surfaces to Off-White (#F9FAFB) background → TC-0150 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0056 All text/background pairs in both themes meet WCAG 4.5:1 contrast for normal text → TC-0151 Pass
Done P1 US-0019

As a developer, I want to change font size, so that I can read comfortably on my device.

S $400.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0057 Pressing A+ in the editor toolbar increments the Monaco font size by 1pt → TC-0152 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0058 Pressing A- in the editor toolbar decrements the Monaco font size by 1pt → TC-0153 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0059 The current font size value is displayed in the toolbar between the A- and A+ buttons → TC-0154 Pass
Done P1 US-0020

As a power user, I want to install extensions, so that I can enhance the IDE with tools I need.

XL $3,200 4/4 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0060 An extension manifest can be registered in ExtensionRegistry and retrieved by id → TC-0156 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0061 activateExtension registers the manifest and returns valid sandbox HTML containing the extension source → TC-0158 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0062 deactivateExtension removes the extension from ExtensionRegistry → TC-0160 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0063 Extensions run in an isolated WebView sandbox — the sandbox HTML wraps source in try/catch → TC-0161 Pass
Done P2 US-0057

As a user, I want an About screen showing the splash and app info, so that I can identify the app version and credits.

S $400.00 4/4 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0149 A ⓘ button in the status bar has a minimum 44pt touch target and is accessible via aria-label="About NomadCode"
  • ✓ AC-0150 Tapping ⓘ opens a full-screen modal displaying the splash image
  • ✓ AC-0151 The About modal footer shows the app version (from package.json) and copyright line
  • ✓ AC-0152 Tapping the ✕ close button dismisses the About modal with a fade animation
Deferred P1 US-0067

As a user, I want the Monaco editor's syntax colours to match my selected NomadCode theme, so that my chosen look (Dracula, Nord, Solarized Light, etc.) is reflected in code colours — not just the app chrome.

M $800.00 0/5 ACs ▾ ⚠ At Risk
  • ○ AC-0202 Each NomadCode theme ID (nomad-dark, one-dark-pro, dracula, monokai, nord, tokyo-night, nomad-light, github-light, solarized-light, catppuccin-latte, night-owl-light) is registered with monaco.editor.defineTheme on editor boot with a full IStandaloneThemeData palette (colors + token rules)
  • ○ AC-0203 getMonacoTheme() returns the registered custom theme name (not vs / vs-dark) so custom colours are applied instead of the Monaco built-in fallback
  • ○ AC-0204 buildMonacoHtml() injects a defineTheme call for every registered theme before the initial setTheme, so the editor never shows a vs-dark flash on custom themes
  • ○ AC-0205 Switching themes in Settings or SetupWizard updates Monaco's active theme within one frame, with no unstyled interval
  • ○ AC-0206 Unit tests confirm each theme's defineTheme payload contains non-empty colors.background, colors.foreground, and at least one token rule for keywords, strings, and comments
Planned P2 US-0100

As a power user, I want to browse and install extensions directly from the VS Code Marketplace inside NomadCode, so that I can discover and add tools without leaving the IDE.

XL $3,200 0/5 ACs ▾ ⚠ At Risk
  • ○ AC-0303 An "Extensions" panel is accessible from the sidebar or command palette, displaying a searchable list of VS Code Marketplace extensions
  • ○ AC-0304 Each extension listing shows name, publisher, rating, download count, description, and a one-tap Install button
  • ○ AC-0305 Installing an extension downloads only the pure-JS / WASM portions of the extension package; any native binary components are silently omitted with an explanatory note (App Store §2.5.2 compliance — no runtime native code download)
  • ○ AC-0306 Installed extensions appear in the Extensions panel under "Installed" and are activated via the existing WASM-sandbox ExtensionHost
  • ○ AC-0307 Extension data (search results, extension metadata) is cached locally for offline browsing; install requires network
EPIC-0006 Done Plan Visualizer MVP (v0.1) $3,200 projected
Done P0 US-0021

As a project lead, I want a plan status dashboard, so that I can see project health at a glance.

XL $3,200 8/8 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0001 HTML page generated from RELEASE_PLAN.md → TC-0001 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0002 Filter by epic, status, priority, search → TC-0010 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0003 Epic→Story→AC→TC hierarchy view → TC-0012 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0004 All 7 Chart.js charts render on Charts tab → TC-0004 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0005 Cost table shows projected and AI costs per story → TC-0008 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0006 Traceability matrix links TCs to stories and ACs → TC-0013 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0007 GitHub Actions auto-deploys to GitHub Pages on qualifying commits
  • ✓ AC-0008 Claude Code stop hook appends cost rows to AI_COST_LOG.md → TC-0030 Pass
EPIC-0007 Done Authentication Release 1.0 (GA) $2,400 projected
Done P0 US-0022

As a developer, I want to sign in with my GitHub account, so that I can access private repositories and push code.

L $1,600 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0064 Tapping "Sign in with GitHub" launches the system browser with the correct OAuth authorize URL
  • ✓ AC-0065 After successful OAuth callback, an access token is returned and stored in the keychain
  • ✓ AC-0066 The user's GitHub username and avatar are displayed in the settings screen after sign-in
Done P0 US-0023

As a developer, I want my auth token stored securely, so that my credentials are never exposed.

S $400.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0067 OAuth access token is stored in the platform keychain (iOS Keychain / Android Keystore), not in app storage or logs
  • ✓ AC-0068 Token survives app restart and is retrieved without re-authentication
Done P1 US-0024

As a developer, I want to sign out, so that I can switch accounts or revoke access.

S $400.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0069 Tapping "Sign out" deletes the token from the keychain and clears the user session
  • ✓ AC-0070 After sign-out, git operations requiring auth prompt re-authentication
EPIC-0008 Done Git Integration Release 1.0 (GA) $4,800 projected
Complete P0 US-0025

As a developer, I want to clone a GitHub repository, so that I can work on an existing project.

L $1,600 5/5 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0071 User can enter a GitHub repository URL and initiate a clone to local storage → TC-0360 Pass
  • ✓ AC-0072 Clone progress is shown via a progress indicator; errors surface a readable message
  • ✓ AC-0073 Cloned repo appears in the file explorer immediately on completion
  • ✓ AC-0191 If the repository requires authentication and no GitHub token is stored, the user sees a clear message and a path to sign in (e.g. Settings)
  • ✓ AC-0192 When the network is unavailable or clone fails transiently, the user sees an actionable message; transient failures retry with exponential backoff up to three times (per AGENTS.md) → TC-0362 Pass
Complete P0 US-0026

As a developer, I want to stage and commit changes, so that I can record my work in git history.

M $800.00 4/4 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0074 Modified files are listed in the git status panel with staged/unstaged indicators
  • ✓ AC-0075 Tapping a file checkbox toggles it between staged and unstaged
  • ✓ AC-0076 Entering a commit message and confirming creates a local commit
  • ✓ AC-0193 Git status panel interactive controls have accessibility labels and minimum 44pt touch targets (WCAG-minded)
Complete P0 US-0027

As a developer, I want to push and pull from remote, so that I can collaborate with my team.

M $800.00 4/4 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0077 Tapping Push sends local commits to the remote using the stored OAuth token
  • ✓ AC-0078 Tapping Pull fetches and merges remote changes into the working directory
  • ✓ AC-0194 If push or pull requires authentication and no GitHub token is stored, the user sees a clear message and a path to sign in (e.g. Settings)
  • ✓ AC-0195 When offline or push/pull fails transiently, the user sees an actionable message; transient failures retry with exponential backoff up to three times (per AGENTS.md)
Complete P1 US-0028

As a developer, I want to view file diffs, so that I can review changes before committing.

M $800.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0079 Tapping a modified file in the git status panel shows an inline diff view
  • ✓ AC-0080 Added lines are highlighted green; removed lines are highlighted red
Complete P1 US-0029

As a developer, I want to create and switch branches, so that I can work on features in isolation.

M $800.00 3/3 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0081 Current branch name is visible in the status bar
  • ✓ AC-0082 User can create a new branch from the current HEAD via the branch panel
  • ✓ AC-0083 User can switch to an existing local branch without losing uncommitted changes

  • ○ AC-0091 While typing, ghost-text completions appear after a 300 ms debounce
  • ○ AC-0092 Pressing Tab accepts the current completion; pressing Escape dismisses it
  • ○ AC-0093 Completions are only triggered for Pro+AI subscribers; other users see no ghost text

  • ○ AC-0095 User can type a prompt referencing the current file and receive a streamed response

  • ○ AC-0100 Build configuration is defined in `eas.json` with a production profile

  • ✓ AC-0153 App icon (1024×1024 NomadCode logo) is correctly referenced in `app.json`, `ios/Images.xcassets/AppIcon`, and all Android mipmap densities (mdpi–xxxhdpi WebP)
  • ✓ AC-0154 Splash screen displays the NomadCode logo centred on Deep Slate (#0F172A) background; `app.json` splash config uses `resizeMode: "contain"` and matching backgroundColor

EPIC-0012 Done Cloud Sync Release 1.1 (Post-Launch) $3,200 projected
Done P1 US-0039

As a developer, I want to upload my project to cloud storage, so that I can access it from multiple devices.

L $1,600 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0105 User can connect an S3-compatible bucket by entering endpoint URL and access credentials in settings
  • ✓ AC-0106 Modified local files are uploaded to the configured bucket automatically on save
Done P1 US-0040

As a developer, I want to download remote changes on launch, so that my files are always up to date.

L $1,600 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0107 On app launch with network access, remote changes are pulled and merged into local storage
  • ✓ AC-0108 Sync conflicts are surfaced to the user with a merge prompt before overwriting local files
EPIC-0013 Done Multi-Language Editor Support Release 1.1 (Post-Launch) $2,400 projected
Done P1 US-0041

As a developer, I want syntax highlighting for Python, Rust, Go, and Swift, so that I can work in my preferred language.

M $800.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0109 Python, Rust, Go, and Swift files receive correct syntax highlighting
  • ✓ AC-0110 Language is auto-detected from file extension; unknown extensions fall back to plain text
Done P2 US-0042

As a developer, I want language-correct indentation and auto-close, so that the editor follows language conventions.

S $400.00 2/2 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0111 Python files default to 4-space indentation; Go files default to tabs
  • ✓ AC-0112 Bracket and quote auto-close is active for all supported languages
Done P0 US-0059

As a developer, I want the editor to apply language-appropriate indentation settings automatically when I open a file, so that code I type is formatted consistently with the conventions of that language.

S $400.00 8/8 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0156 Python files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=true, detectIndentation=false
  • ✓ AC-0157 Go files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=false (hard tabs per gofmt)
  • ✓ AC-0158 Rust and Swift files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=true
  • ✓ AC-0159 C, C++, C#, F#, Java, and Kotlin files use tabSize=4, insertSpaces=true
  • ✓ AC-0160 TypeScript and JavaScript files use tabSize=2, insertSpaces=true
  • ✓ AC-0161 Dart, HTML, CSS, JSON, YAML, and Markdown files use tabSize=2, insertSpaces=true
  • ✓ AC-0162 detectIndentation is always false — Monaco must not override configured rules
  • ✓ AC-0163 When switching tabs, indentation rules for the new tab's language are applied within the same message cycle as the content load
Done P1 US-0060

As a developer, I want language-specific auto-close bracket behaviour, so that bracket and quote completion matches the language I am writing.

S $400.00 4/4 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0164 Python files use autoClosingQuotes='languageDefined' (defers to Monaco's built-in Python grammar to avoid doubling quotes inside strings)
  • ✓ AC-0165 Ruby, R, HTML, and XML files use autoClosingQuotes='languageDefined'
  • ✓ AC-0166 All other supported languages use autoClosingBrackets='always' and autoClosingQuotes='always'
  • ✓ AC-0167 Switching from a Python tab to a Go tab updates autoClosingQuotes from 'languageDefined' to 'always'
Done P1 US-0061

As a developer working with Zig, Dart, Lua, Elixir, C#, F#, R, Scala, Perl, PowerShell, Terraform, or Protocol Buffers files, I want NomadCode to recognise those extensions and apply a Monaco language mode and correct indentation.

S $400.00 4/4 ACs ▾
  • ✓ AC-0168 .cs → csharp, .fs/.fsx → fsharp, .zig → zig, .dart → dart, .lua → lua, .ex/.exs → elixir, .r/.R → r, .scala → scala, .pl/.pm → perl, .ps1/.psm1 → powershell, .tf/.hcl → hcl, .proto → proto, .ini/.cfg/.env → ini
  • ✓ AC-0169 Extension matching is case-insensitive (e.g. App.DART → dart)
  • ✓ AC-0170 Vue SFCs (.vue) fall back to html language mode (no dedicated Monaco grammar)
  • ✓ AC-0171 jsonc files map to Monaco language ID 'jsonc'; toml files map to 'toml'

  • ○ AC-0114 Entering a query returns all matching lines across the project file tree → TC-0347 Pass
